title: ampelocissus tomentosa
intro: Ampelocissus tomentosa, also known as the climbing vine or hairy grape vine, is a plant species in the grape family (Vitaceae) native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have shown potential in treating various health conditions. The health benefits of Ampelocissus tomentosa include reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and acting as an antimicrobial agent. Herbal preparations of Ampelocissus tomentosa often include infusions, decoctions, or tinctures made from the plant's leaves, stems, and roots, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
